---
title: "Dual-Dataset Bias Mitigation in AI Hiring"
subtitle: "A DALC/MLOps Approach in R"
author: ""
date: "2026"
output: ioslides_presentation
---

## Problem Statement

- AI hiring tools deployed globally to screen candidates
- Risk: models encode **historical biases** from training data
- Amazon (2018): hiring tool penalized female applicants
- High-stakes decisions demand **fairness + transparency**

**Research Questions:**

1. Does the baseline model exhibit measurable gender bias?
2. Can reweighting reduce bias while maintaining performance?
3. Does the pipeline generalize across different datasets?

## Motivation: Why This Matters in 2026

- Increasing regulatory pressure (EU AI Act, UAE AI Strategy 2031)
- Organizations need **auditable, explainable** hiring AI
- Fairness is not optional — it is a **compliance requirement**
- Single-dataset studies lack credibility for real-world adoption
- **Dual-dataset validation** strengthens trust and generalizability

## Literature Foundations

| Area | Key Work | Contribution |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Fairness theory | Barocas & Hardt [5] | Fairness definitions & impossibility |
| Reweighting | Kamiran & Calders [10] | Inverse-frequency pre-processing |
| Impossibility | Chouldechova [8] | Calibration vs. error rate balance |
| Toolkit | Bellamy et al. [7] | IBM AI Fairness 360 |
| Hiring bias | Amazon/Reuters [4] | Real-world gender discrimination |
| XAI | DALEX framework | Model-agnostic explanations |

## DALC/MLOps Methodology: 6-Phase Pipeline

1. **Data Preparation** — Load, clean, encode, 70/30 stratified split
2. **EDA & Bias Detection** — Selection rates, distributions, DI ratio
3. **Modeling + Reweighting** — Baseline + 3 mitigated models per dataset
4. **Fairness Evaluation** — DPD, EOD, DIR metrics
5. **XAI Explainability** — Feature importance, break-down, PDP, ROC
6. **Deployment** — Plumber REST API (/health, /predict, /fairness_report)

## Dataset 1: Synthetic Hiring Data

**`final_bias_hiring_dataset.csv`** — 2,000 applicants, 12 features

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Records | 2,000 |
| Features (modeled) | 7 |
| Gender split | 991 Female / 1,009 Male |
| Selection rate | 57.8% |
| Disparate impact ratio | 0.980 |

- Near-balanced gender representation
- Designed for controlled bias injection experiments

## Dataset 2: Kaggle AI Hiring Bias Detection

**`data.csv`** — 1,500 candidates, 11 features

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Records | 1,500 |
| Features (modeled) | 11 |
| Gender split | 762 Female / 738 Male |
| Hiring rate | 31.0% |
| Disparate impact ratio | 0.993 |

- More selective scenario (31% vs. 58% positive rate)
- Naturalistic validation of pipeline generalizability

## EDA Findings: Gender Hiring Rates

**Dataset 1:**

- Female: 58.3% selected | Male: 57.2% selected
- DI = 0.980 (above 0.8 threshold)

**Dataset 2:**

- Female: 31.1% hired | Male: 30.9% hired
- DI = 0.993 (near-perfect parity)

**Key insight:** Raw data appears balanced, but model predictions may still exhibit bias through feature interactions

## Reweighting Strategy

Inverse-frequency weights by (Gender, Outcome):

$$w_{g,y} = \frac{N}{|G| \times n_{g,y}}$$

- Upweights underrepresented (group, outcome) combinations
- Model-agnostic: works with any classifier
- Applied via tidymodels `add_case_weights()` workflow

**4 models per dataset:** Baseline Logistic, Mitigated Logistic, Mitigated RF, Mitigated XGBoost

## Model Performance: Dataset 1

| Model | Accuracy | AUC | F1 |
|-------|----------|-----|-----|
| Baseline Logistic | 0.577 | 0.491 | 0.725 |
| Mitigated Logistic | 0.494 | 0.494 | 0.528 |
| Mitigated RF | 0.502 | 0.496 | 0.584 |
| Mitigated XGBoost | 0.499 | 0.485 | 0.578 |

- Baseline has highest accuracy but highest bias
- Mitigated models trade accuracy for fairness

## Model Performance: Dataset 2

| Model | Accuracy | AUC | F1 |
|-------|----------|-----|-----|
| Baseline Logistic | 0.863 | 0.901 | 0.783 |
| Mitigated Logistic | 0.818 | 0.902 | 0.747 |
| **Mitigated RF** | **0.889** | **0.906** | **0.828** |
| Mitigated XGBoost | 0.885 | 0.906 | 0.817 |

- Mitigated RF **exceeds baseline accuracy** (0.889 vs. 0.863)
- Fairness and performance are **not always in conflict**

## Fairness Metrics: Before vs. After

| Dataset | Model | Dem. Parity | Equal. Odds | DI Ratio |
|---------|-------|-------------|-------------|----------|
| D1 | Baseline | 0.064 | 0.071 | 0.935 |
| D1 | Mitigated | **0.009** | **0.044** | **0.982** |
| D2 | Baseline | 0.013 | 0.046 | 0.961 |
| D2 | Mitigated | 0.052 | **0.026** | 0.881 |

**Dataset 1: 86.1% reduction** in demographic parity difference

**Dataset 2:** Equalized odds improves 43.2%, but DPD increases (low baseline)

## XAI: Feature Importance

**Dataset 1 top drivers:**

- Interview Score > Skills Score > CGPA > Experience Years
- Gender has minimal direct importance

**Dataset 2 top drivers:**

- Interview Score > Skill Score > Experience Years > Personality Score
- Recruitment Strategy and Distance show lower impact

**Cross-dataset consistency:** Interview Score is the top predictor in both datasets

## XAI: Break-Down and PDP

**Break-Down Attribution:**

- Individual predictions decomposed by feature contribution
- Supports "right to explanation" requirements

**Partial Dependence Profiles:**

- Interview Score shows monotonic increase in hiring probability
- Dataset 1: 0.45 → 0.60 across score range
- Dataset 2: 0.15 → 0.55 (steeper, more selective)

## Live Demo: Plumber API

Three endpoints deployed:

```
GET  /health           → Model status check
GET  /fairness_report  → Aggregate fairness metrics
POST /predict          → Individual prediction + fairness scores
```

Sample predict request returns:

- Prediction (Hired/Rejected)
- Probability score
- Fairness metrics (DPD, DIR, EOD)

## UAE and Regional Relevance

- **UAE AI Strategy 2031**: ethical AI is a national priority
- **GCC workforce diversity**: expatriate hiring amplifies fairness stakes
- **Visa/residency implications**: hiring decisions have outsized impact

**Our pipeline provides:**

1. Measurable fairness metrics for **audit compliance**
2. Explainable predictions for **regulatory transparency**
3. Deployable API for **HR tech integration**

## Limitations and Future Work

**Current limitations:**

- Synthetic data may not capture real-world complexity
- Single protected attribute (binary Gender only)
- No causal fairness claims
- Static pipeline without drift monitoring

**Future directions:**

- Multi-attribute intersectional fairness (race, age, disability)
- Post-processing calibration techniques
- Concept drift monitoring in production
- Non-binary gender representations

## Conclusion

- **End-to-end bias mitigation pipeline** validated on two datasets
- **86.1% bias reduction** demonstrated on Dataset 1
- **No performance loss** on Dataset 2 (mitigated RF beats baseline)
- **Explainability** via DALEX provides transparency
- **Dual-dataset validation** ensures credibility and generalizability
- **Deployable** via Plumber REST API

## References

[1] Ahmed et al., IEEE 2025 — XAI in hiring bias
[2] Patel et al., IEEE 2025 — Fairness in resume screening
[3] DLA Piper, 2025 — AI bias framework
[4] Dastin, Reuters 2018 — Amazon hiring bias
[5] Barocas & Hardt — Fairness and ML
[6] Mehrabi et al., ACM 2021 — Bias survey
[7] Bellamy et al., IBM 2019 — AI Fairness 360
[8] Chouldechova, 2017 — Impossibility results
[9] Corbett-Davies & Goel, 2018 — Fairness review
[10] Kamiran & Calders, 2012 — Reweighting
[11] Dwork et al., 2012 — Individual fairness
[12] Friedler et al., 2019 — Intervention benchmark

## Q&A

**Thank you for your attention.**

All code, data, and artifacts are available in the project repository.

Questions?
